As Edith and Sandra hurry to Phil's house, Edith receives a phone call from her 'difficult' sister Joan inviting herself to stay. Sandra pulls faces, but Edith feels obliged to agree. The duo enter Phil's house where they find a piano-playing Queenie, but no Phil. Roger visits the recovering Bob in hospital and tells him that he has thought of a new way to make money and he has let his mother's spare room, online, to two Americans. programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern try to help the Miller family in North Lincolnshire, with parents Wes and Mel wanting to save up for their wedding after 10 years together. With no savings and big expectations, they need to make considerable changes if they want the wedding of their dreams - can Alex and Steph show these big spenders that it is possible to create memories without breaking the bank, and help them put money aside for their special day? The Great British public gets their say too, as they put four similar-looking biker jackets to the test(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate what could turn out to be one of the best bargains ever seen on the programme. Almost 30 years ago, Bob Kay bought a junk shop still life for £1. If it turns out to be by Giorgio de Chirico, it could be worth up to £50,000. Fiona and Philip visit Rome, where de Chirico lived for most of his life, and while they learn a great deal about the painter's status as a master of 20th-century art, they also discover he is widely faked. However, they do come across some interesting documents with which to begin their investigation(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Britain's betting industry is booming, with the amount people gamble having almost doubled in a decade and company profits soaring to £14.5 billion. But are the bookies doing enough to protect problem gamblers? Reporter Bronagh Munro investigates an industry where complaints are rising even more quickly than profits, meeting one person who gambled away more than £3 million, and families who have lost their children to addiction.(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Oscar-winning actress Kate Winslet follows a rumour of Scandinavian ancestry on her late mother Sally's side of the family and is delighted when it turns out to be true and that she has Swedish heritage. However, her joy turns to tears and anger when she uncovers the extreme hardships her ancestors endured, from famine to flogging and imprisonment. Switching to her father's side of the family, Kate finds a drummer boy in the Grenadier Guards and, this time, an unusually positive encounter with prison(n)

programme.title: Mustangs FC
programme.desc: The team has made it to the final but it feels like nobody has faith in them(n)

programme.title: Deadly Top 10
programme.desc: Steve Backshall chooses his favourite creatures that live in extreme environments, including the snow leopard and a deep-diving whale(n)

programme.title: Penguins on a Plane: Great Animal Moves
programme.desc: Despite the ratings-grabbing title, this two-parter isn't quite the action thriller it sounds. Rather, it's an insightful documentary revealing how thousands of exotic animals are moved around the world every day, and how they cope with being transported. First up, a flock of gentoo penguins get a luxury £40,000 container for their trip from New Zealand to Birmingham, while staff at the West Midlands Safari Park prepare to bid farewell to Pinky the two-ton hippo, who puts her trip to the south of France in danger by testing a reinforced crate to its limits. Plus, a beekeeper awaits a precious cargo of some eight million bees, which he hopes will help British fruit farmers(n)

programme.title: Cats v Dogs: Which Is Best?
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Liz Bonnin present the second of two programmes in which pooches and pussies are put through a number of tests to see which family pet comes out on top. There are five more rounds, concentrating on which animal is easiest to train, communication, which is best at helping reduce people's stress, independence and the big question - do cats and dogs really love their owners? On the search for answers, dog-lover Chris meets a Border Collie which can recognise more than 150 words and Liz meets a cat capable of choosing its own TV channels(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Lynn and Sandra in the Highlands get a surprise when they harvest their first batch of honey. The Rodgers family gather to give bull Mario a good send-off, while Raymond and Johnny discover their sheep are under threat from flystrike, and the only way to protect them is to put the entire flock of 1,600 through the sheepdip(n)

programme.title: Lorraine Pascale: How to Be a Better Cook
programme.desc: Bryony Crutcher, 26, admits to passing off shop-bought food as her own creations, but now she's been asked to host her best friend's hen party and needs Lorraine Pascale's help to come up with an impressive meal. The food writer puts together an inspiring canape menu for Bryony to master, including Thai larb gai, bite-sized chocolate Napoleons and delicate coconut meringues filled with ginger cream(n)

programme.title: Secret Britain
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Adam Henson explore the moors of North Yorkshire. Adam meets a farmer whose father uncovered a Roman villa while ploughing, attempts to make a clay pot using 2,000-year-old technology and discovers the remains of a forgotten glass-making industry. Ellie finds out why Britain's highest pub, the Tan Hill Inn, was built miles away from the nearest customers, investigates the Whitby Yards - former slums that have become highly sought as havens of peace - and visits the ruins of Rievaulx Abbey(n)

programme.title: Animals at Play
programme.desc: Part two of two. The secrets behind animals' group games, from dolphins that play catch to a hyena tug of war, revealing the benefits they gain from their playful behaviour. The programme examines how breaching the rules of a game can have fatal consequences - but how it also presents an opportunity for animals with a rigid hierarchy to break down social barriers(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the second and penultimate day of the biennial event, held this year at Zdzislaw Krzyszkowiak Stadium in Bydgoszcz, Poland. Events taking place today include the men's and women's 100m and 400m finals, as well as the men's 1500m and women's 3,000m, with Great Britain and holders Germany among the 12 teams involved(n)

programme.title: Proms Encore
programme.desc: Katie Derham is joined by composer Hannah Kendall and trombonist Peter Moore as Proms Encore celebrates the next generation of classical musicians. Rachel Parris discovers what makes the new stars tick, Tom Service takes a musical trip with a maestro and soprano Fatma Said gives a special performance on the bandstand(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.sub-title: The Two and a Half Feathers
programme.desc: Jones's glorious military reputation is called into question when new platoon member George Clarke makes scandalous allegations about his conduct at the Battle of Omdurman in Sudan. Comedy first aired in 1970, starring Arthur Lowe, Clive Dunn and John Le Mesurier(n)

credits.actor: Arthur Lowe, Clive Dunn, John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Princess Margaret: The Rebel Royal
programme.desc: Part two of two. Experts analyse the Princess's marriage to Lord Snowdon at the start of the 1960s. At home with celebrities and artists, they're riding the wave of a cultural and sexual revolution that's transforming Britain. The mystique of monarchy is being increasingly undermined by the emergence of a more open and egalitarian society(n)

programme.title: Don't Look Back
programme.desc: DA Pennebaker's superb, intimate film diary of Bob Dylan's 1965 tour of Britain, chronicling reactions as his tunes caught the nation off-guard, but ultimately won him critical acclaim and worldwide fame and fortune. Featuring interviews with various Sixties cult figures, including the man himself, his then-girlfriend Joan Baez and poet Allen Ginsberg(n)

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Paper Boi is quick to regret his decision to play a show at a college campus that Earn arranged. Comedy drama about a rapper and his manager(n)

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: A flashback to Earn and Al's adolescence, where the authenticity of Earn's shirt is embarrassingly called into question at school(n)

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Earn runs around town trying to finish everything before catching a plane so that his rapper cousin Alfred, aka Paper Boi, can go on a European tour with Clark County. Comedy drama, starring Donald Glover, about an aspiring music manager trying to turn his life around by making his rapper cousin a star(n)

programme.title: Clouds of Sils Maria
programme.desc: A famous actress prepares to star in a new production of the play that made her career in a supporting part, with the lead role now played by a scandalous Hollywood star. She and her assistant head to a cottage in the Swiss mountains to prepare, which leads her to reflect on the harsh truths about how her life has changed. Drama, starring Juliette Binoche, Kristen Stewart and Chloe Grace Moretz(n)

credits.actor: Juliette Binoche, Kristen Stewart, Chloe Grace Moretz

programme.title: Stations of the Cross
programme.desc: A girl from a strict Catholic family is an outsider in her secular school, and faces constant reproach from her fundamentalist mother. Under the tuition of a misguided priest, she pushes herself to extreme acts of self-sacrifice in her pursuit of spiritual redemption. Drama, starring Lea van Acken and Florian Stetter. In German, French and Latin(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden explores Hampshire by hot-air balloon, beginning at Mottisfont Abbey in Romsey, where she meets a gardening veteran who is preparing to hand over his duties to a young protegee. She visits the Sir Harold Hillier Gardens, and also climbs into a pair of waders for a go at an unusual kind of weeding(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty advises on essential summer pruning and adds French beans to the vegetable garden. The programme also meets a gardener in Hertfordshire who combines her love of cottage garden plants with tropical plants and visits a garden in Kent where pots are the stars of the summer show(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team heads to Norfolk, where John Craven meets the team behind a corncrake breeding programme, while Margherita Taylor meets artist Shauna Richardson and a sheepdog trainer who also breeds red squirrels. Tom Heap looks at how devices designed to scare seals away from salmon stocks could be having unintended consequences and Adam Henson is in Kent to meets a young shepherdess making a name for herself in the sheepdog handling world(n)

programme.title: The Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Kirsty shows how to make a miniature patio pond, Janie Gall demonstrates an easy cut flower arrangement and George visits an award-winning garden in Livingston, West Lothian(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Nisha Katona takes Fred Sirieix to San Sebastian for a mind-blowing meal at one of the world's top restaurants and to visit a tiny sports bar to learn the secrets of the perfect tortilla. The first place in Nisha's little black book is Mugaritz, ranked as the ninth best restaurant in the world and famous for wild experimentation - something that is made clear as soon as the menus arrive. Instead of a list of dishes to select, the Mugaritz menu is two pictures, one of a swimming fish, one of a flying fish, and Nisha and Fred make their choice, but have little clue what they are going to eat(n)

programme.title: Top of the Shop with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: In the second heat, four food producers with fledgling businesses come to test out their products on the locals in Malhamdale, in the heart of the Yorkshire Dales. This episode sees cheese makers from across the country - Gillian, Jo, Pedro and Julie - compete against each other for a place in the final(n)

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti work at Giraffe Manor in Kenya, a unique hotel where giraffe, staff and guests co-exist in a 1930s Scottish-style hunting lodge on the edge of Nairobi National Park. The pair travel to the remote but luxurious outpost of Sasaab Lodge, a six-acre plot set within 82,000 acres of land owned by Samburu tribespeople, who receive a fee for every guest that stays and make up 75 per cent of the staff(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the third and final day of the biennial event, held this year at Zdzislaw Krzyszkowiak Stadium in Bydgoszcz, Poland. Events taking place today include men's 110m hurdles and 800m and women's 1,500m and 5,000m, before proceedings are brought to a close in the traditional manner with the men's and women's 4x100m and 4x400m relays(n)

programme.title: International Rugby Union
programme.desc: England v Wales. John Inverdale presents action from both teams' first World Cup warm-up match, held at Twickenham. Wales prevailed 21-13 when the sides met at Principality Stadium in this year's Six Nations and went on to win the Grand Slam. With analysis from Jonathan Davies and Jeremy Guscott(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a clothing factory in South Shields that manufactures 650 waxed jackets a day. Cherry Healey investigates the science behind waterproof clothing, discovering how a layer of material can keep rain in while allowing sweat to escape, as well as discovering 150-year-old methods of making umbrellas. Plus, Ruth Goodman visits a Scottish harbour to discover the origins of weatherproof coats(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, founder of Crafter's Companion, is a new addition to the panel on the business ideas contest, joining returning dragons Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Tej Lalvani in putting more would-be entrepreneurs' ideas to the test. Making their pitches this week are the creator of a brand of spiced rum, who gives the studio an exciting new look, a couple who have brought their four-legged friend with them to demonstrate a range of pet food, and the inventor of a special toilet gadget. Evan Davis presents(n)

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan visits Bosnia and Herzegovina to discover how this small corner of Eastern Europe has changed since the war, with the aid of a local museum curator. He tries out Sarajevo's abandoned bobsleigh track, a remnant of the 1984 winter Olympics, stays in a 'war hostel', and meets a man who claims to have discovered the world's largest pyramid(n)

programme.title: The Mind of Herbert Clunkerdunk
programme.desc: Herbert gets an important job recording a voiceover, but Bobby is less than impressed by the discovery that he has spent all of their savings on glow-in-the-dark paint. The couple is also called in for an urgent parent-teacher meeting at their son's school, which leads Herbert to reminisce about some unpleasant childhood memories(n)

programme.title: The Mind of Herbert Clunkerdunk
programme.desc: Herbert poses for some flashy new headshots to go in his portfolio, in the hope than one of them will land him an acting job, and he can begin paying for all the things he has been buying on the internet - and hopefully leave enough to pay the council tax bill. However, a bicycle ride and an overlong bath have unforeseen consequences(n)

programme.title: Louis Theroux's Altered States
programme.sub-title: Louis Theroux's Altered States: Take My Baby
programme.desc: The documentary-maker explores how adoption is a big business in California, meeting agencies, facilitators and lawyers matching babies to parents, with the adoption process often costing as much as $50,000. He meets families that have spent vast sums on children they are unable to conceive themselves, and women preparing to give up their babies, hearing the stories of poverty, addiction and abuse that have inspired their decision. He also discovers how the adoption process is open to abuse, and reflects on what happens when a mother has a post-birth change of heart(n)

programme.title: Killed By My Debt
programme.desc: Factual drama telling the story of Jerome Rogers and the destructive power of debt. In January 2015, the south London 19-year-old finally got what he'd been working for - a new motorbike and his first real job as a courier. However, in the hands of bailiffs, two £65 traffic fines rose to over a thousand pounds. Some weeks, take-home pay in his zero-hours job was as low as £12. Under the pressure of his debt, Jerome went to some woods and committed suicide. With Chance Perdomo(n)

programme.title: A Fresh Guide to Florence with Fab 5 Freddy
programme.desc: Hip-hop pioneer Fred Brathwaite, better known to music fans as Fab 5 Freddy, who was part of a creative revolution during the 1970s and 1980s, goes back in time to the 15th and 16th centuries to examine the multi-racial society that played a part in the Italian Renaissance but have seemingly fallen through the cracks of history. With so many Renaissance artists representing black figures of prominence in their work, Freddy aims to bring focus back to the social change at the heart of the art movement and artists who were the cultural disrupters of their time(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac is forced to make a career-changing decision when she is reunited with her daughter, who has been brought to the hospital by her ex's new fiancee. Cameron resists talking about his mother, while Serena's tough facade cracks as she seeks solace in an unlikely source. Dominic becomes convinced that something is going on between Carole and Hanssen, and his obsession brings him closer to learning the truth(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Cruise: A Life at Sea
programme.desc: Documentary following life aboard the cruise ship Balmoral during its 112-day journey around the world, with 1,700 passengers and crew setting off from Southampton in January. A sick holidaymaker has to be airlifted off the ship in the middle of the Atlantic, while choir mistress Sue Alderson drills her volunteers for a performance of The Pirates of Penzance at the end of the leg in Acapulco(n)

programme.title: The Cruise: A Life at Sea
programme.desc: The Balmoral crosses the Pacific on the second leg of her round-the-world cruise, with a stop on the island of Tahiti along the way. Drama producer Carol Bunyan is hard at work with her volunteer thespians, Ian and Gail Hussey celebrate their 50th wedding anniversary by renewing their vows with the help of the Rev Colin Still, and there is tragedy when a passenger dies(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble meet the newest diva at the Park - Inny the giant otter, while Jean Johansson discovers why koalas can sometimes hamper Australian detectives. Plus, the Park is placed on red alert as one of the carnivores goes missing(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a cherry Bakewell factory in Stoke on Trent that produces 250,000 tarts every day, and meets the team of 12 employed to precisely place the cherries on top by hand. Cherry Healey learns the tricks of avoiding a soggy pastry bottom when baking at home, and visits a factory where almonds are roasted and milled to make butter. Historian Ruth Goodman discovers the origins of frangipane as a perfume in 17th-century Paris, and discovers how the modern cherry Bakewell recipe was arrived at by accident(n)

programme.title: BBC News at 9
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts the panel show that tests the wildlife knowledge of team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke and their guests Christy Harrison and Niall Strawson(n)

programme.title: Getting the Builders In
programme.desc: Donna desperately needs an office revamp in Sandhurst and Karl has a £12,000 budget to overhaul his garden in Urmston, Greater Manchester(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott and Len Goodman head to Bradford, where the Strictly Come Dancing judge has his first taste of a Pakistani curry and samples some Latvian cuisine(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King explore the history behind the boom in pub food, tracing it from the humble beginnings of bar snacks and chicken-in-a-basket to the birth of the gastro pub. The duo also recreate some mouthwatering dishes that would make any publican proud(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Vet Steve helps a kestrel with a damaged wing get back into the wild, while equine specialist Sybil treats a pony with a locked leg - and a reputation for misbehaviour. At the Small Animal Hospital in Fife, heart and lung expert Craig is under pressure as he attempts to save a Yorkshire terrier's life. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Life Story
programme.desc: David Attenborough's documentary charting the circle of life in the animal kingdom ends with creatures' attempts to rear their offspring, revealing the commitment involved and how for some parents it means risking their own lives. A turtle hauls itself up the beach to lay its eggs in a safe place, only for the tide to trap it behind a wall of coral. A mother bonobo in the Congo teaches its son the skills it will need to survive adulthood, bison protect their calves from the ever-present threat of wolves and a mother zebra has to lead its foal across a dangerous river(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Philip Serrell and Louise Gostelow set off on a search for items on a journey through East Anglia, heading for an auction showdown in Colchester(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Musical comedian Mitch Benn, wine expert Joe Fattorini, broadcaster Samira Ahmed,sports presenter Steve Rider and writer Emma Kennedy take on the general-knowledge champions, hoping to win a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: The consumers unbox, test and score a science kit, a pet seat carpet and a make-up cleanser and applicator. Lucy Alexander presents(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore four more treasured family heirlooms, tackling a musical wooden bench, a Victorian kaleidoscope, military items owned by a soldier who served in both world wars and a pair of Royal Crown Derby peacocks(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Time to Eat
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares the recipes she relies on when she hasn't got the energy to cook anything complicated, including French toast spiced with turmeric, falafel burgers, and crispy apple pies. Nadiya also heads to the Heinz factory in Wigan to find out how the team meet the public's demand for baked beans, while she joins a busy vet during lambing season to help him reinvigorate his lunchtimes(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The fifth first-round match sees an Oxbridge clash as four students from Wolfson College, Cambridge pit their wits against a team from St John's College, Oxford in a bid to reach the second stage. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: When Bridges Collapse: The Genoa Disaster
programme.desc: On August 14,2018, the Ponte Morandi in Genoa collapsed, sending 27 vehicles plummeting to the valley below with the loss of 43 lives. This investigation into the disaster tells the stories of people on the bridge that day and examines what caused the road viaduct to fail so catastrophically(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: The Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles look back over the year the high-kicking heroes in a half-shell took the world by storm, Bill and Ted set off on an Excellent Adventure and Debbie Gibson went head to head with Tiffany for pop supremacy. This Morning began dominating daytime television, Bros and Neighbours star Kylie Minogue hit the charts, and the emergence of acid house changed the clubbing scene(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Tribes, Predators & Me
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan travels across Ethiopia to find out how people can live so close to hyenas, powerful carnivores that weigh up to 14 stone and are capable of killing lions in packs. He starts his journey in the capital Addis Ababa, where hyenas prowl the outskirts for food and are tolerated because people believe they ward off the devil. He also meets cattle-herders who dislike hyenas because of attacks on their livestock, and visits the medieval city of Harar, which has a reputation for having hundreds of hyenas, many of which inhabit the local graveyard(n)

programme.title: Lost Land of the Volcano
programme.desc: Scientists and wildlife film-makers led by Steve Backshall, George McGavin and Gordon Buchanan explore Mount Bosavi, a giant extinct volcano covered by thick and largely unexplored rainforest in New Guinea. With the help of trackers from a remote tribe, they search for rare creatures and species to find evidence that may help stop the area's trees being felled for timber. The nest of the world's smallest parrot, an unusual beetle and new types of frog, gecko and bat are among the team's first discoveries(n)

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates the story of some of New Zealand's wildest places, home to hyper-intelligent parrots, snails with teeth, and constellations of glow-worms. In these often inhospitable locations, any creature hoping to survive must resort to radical techniques, none more so than on the South Island, which lies toward Antarctica and is home to the rapidly rising peaks of the Southern Alps(n)

programme.title: Below the Surface
programme.desc: June's kidnappers threaten to kill the ferry's passengers unless she gives herself up, while the authorities, alerted by a silent alarm, prepare a plan of action. In Danish(n)

programme.title: Below the Surface
programme.desc: Yusuf warns Danish intelligence that if any attempt is made to rescue the ferry hostages they will all be killed. Back on shore, SP and Simon are visited by Mahdi's wife. In Danish(n)

programme.title: The Vietnam War
programme.desc: Ten years in the making, this series tells the epic story of the Vietnam War, bringing the war and the chaotic epoch it encompassed viscerally to life. In this opening part, Vietnamese revolutionaries led by Ho Chi Minh end nearly a century of French colonial occupation and, with the Cold War intensifying, Vietnam is divided in two at Geneva(n)

programme.title: The Vietnam War
programme.desc: President Kennedy and his advisers wrestle with how deeply to get involved in South Vietnam, as the increasingly autocratic Diem regime faces a growing communist insurgency(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Nicky Campbell introduce the edition from April 24, featuring Pat and Mick, James Brown, Fairground Attraction, Brenda Russell, the Primitives, Luther Vandross, Joyce Sims, Bananarama, Will Downing, S'Express and Scott Fitzgerald(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes and Adrian John introduce the edition first aired on May 5, featuring performances by Joyce Sims, New Order, Magnum, Harry Enfield, the Adventures, Narada, Prince, the Christians, the Primitives, S'Express and Star Turn on 45 Pints(n)

programme.title: Lost Land of the Volcano
programme.desc: Scientists and wildlife film-makers led by Steve Backshall, George McGavin and Gordon Buchanan explore Mount Bosavi, a giant extinct volcano covered by thick and largely unexplored rainforest in New Guinea. With the help of trackers from a remote tribe, they search for rare creatures and species to find evidence that may help stop the area's trees being felled for timber. The nest of the world's smallest parrot, an unusual beetle and new types of frog, gecko and bat are among the team's first discoveries(n)

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates the story of some of New Zealand's wildest places, home to hyper-intelligent parrots, snails with teeth, and constellations of glow-worms. In these often inhospitable locations, any creature hoping to survive must resort to radical techniques, none more so than on the South Island, which lies toward Antarctica and is home to the rapidly rising peaks of the Southern Alps(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: Love and loss, life and death collide in a version of Mozart's Requiem by Nathalie Stutzmann and the BBC National Orchestra and Chorus of Wales, featuring soloists Fatma Said, Kathryn Rudge, Sunnyboy Dladla and David Shipley, from the Royal Albert Hall(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: Horizon: Tim Peake Special - How to Be an Astronaut
programme.desc: Highlights of a video diary filmed over two years by Tim Peake as he prepared to become the first British astronaut on board the International Space Station(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.sub-title: The Sky at Night Guides: Galaxies
programme.desc: Chris Lintott uncovers the deepest secrets of the galaxies. Using the Sky at Night archives he explores the first galaxy ever discovered, today's cutting-edge methods to map the Milky Way and goes beyond to the edge of the universe(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The programme goes behind the scenes as the European Space Agency selects its next mission, which will be launched in 2028, meeting British teams vying to have their ideas selected, including a revolutionary mission to a comet(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines the social significance of cars in the 20th century. He begins by travelling to Germany, Italy and Russia to examine how dictators kick-started the mobilization of the masses, finding tales of design brilliance, abject failure, war, fraud and double dealing. James also learns about how the British motor industry blew a gift-wrapped chance to rule the world and he gets his own back with a stunt that means bad news for one of the planet's most hated cars(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary about the final joint assignment of war correspondent Marie Colvin and photographer Paul Conroy to cover the siege of Homs in Syria in 2012, during which Colvin was killed by artillery fire. The film tells the story of the pair's fateful mission and Conroy's epic battle to escape the city to tell the world of his fallen colleague and the plight of the people of Homs(n)

programme.title: Perfect Pianists at the BBC
programme.desc: David Owen Norris trawls through 60 years of BBC archive material to showcase some of the greatest names in the history of the piano. From the groundbreaking studio recitals of Benno Moiseiwitsch, Solomon and Myra Hess in the 1950s, through the legendary concerts of Vladimir Horowitz and Arthur Rubinstein to more recent performances including Alfred Brendel, Mitsuko Uchida and Stephen Hough, David celebrates some of the greatest players in a pianistic tradition that goes back to Franz Liszt in the 19th century(n)

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Part one of two. Actor Richard E Grant follows in the footsteps of artists who lived and painted on France's Cote d'Azur, renowned for its sunny climate. The first edition focuses on the relationship between modern art and the location's development as an international tourist haven. Cezanne, Monet and Renoir were captivated by the light and colour of the landscape in the 19th century and their resultant work advertised the region's beauty. Meanwhile, the area's vivid hues inspired Henri Matisse to adopt a new palette that set modern art on the route to abstraction(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.sub-title: The Sky at Night Guides: Galaxies
programme.desc: Chris Lintott uncovers the deepest secrets of the galaxies. Using the Sky at Night archives he explores the first galaxy ever discovered, today's cutting-edge methods to map the Milky Way and goes beyond to the edge of the universe(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Snowdonia
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores Snowdonia through the seasons, beginning during spring in Nant Ffrancon in the Ogwen Valley. A mountain guide takes him high up above the glacial landscape of Cwm Idwal, before the wildlife expert heads to a hidden river gorge in full flow, listens to the dawn chorus in an ancient woodland, and visits Bird Rock, an imposing crag in the Dysynni Valley(n)

programme.title: Operation Grand Canyon with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Part two of two. The historian and his team continue down the Colorado River in antique wooden boats as they re-create the pioneering 280-mile expedition through the Grand Canyon that was led by explorer John Wesley Powell in 1869. Along the way they carry out the same surveying tasks as the original party with period equipment, including taking photographs with a 19th-century 3D camera(n)

programme.title: Deep Ocean
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a documentary following a crew attempting to film bioluminescent creatures in a huge underwater canyon in California's Monterey Bay(n)

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to marine invertebrates - bizarre and beautiful animals that thrive in the toughest parts of the ocean. Divers swim into a shoal of predatory Humboldt squid emerging from the depths to hunt in packs, and timelapse photography reveals starfish gathering under the Arctic ice to devour a seal carcass. A camera also follows a female octopus into a cave, where she hides to protect her eggs until she starves to death(n)

programme.title: Farther and Sun: A Dyslexic Road Trip
programme.desc: Film-maker Richard Macer examines the condition with his dyslexic son, prior to being tested for the condition himself. Their journey brings them into contact with successful dyslexic people, including Richard Branson and Eddie Izzard, uncovers the challenges dyslexic people face in life, and visit companies that are actively seeking dyslexic staff, viewing the condition as a potential advantage(n)

programme.title: How the Celts Saved Britain
programme.desc: Part one of two. Dan Snow unravels the mystery of the lost years of 400-800AD, looking at the demise of civilisation in Britain following the collapse of the Roman Empire, and revealing how Christianity transformed Ireland into a cultural powerhouse(n)

programme.title: Arthur

programme.desc: A new sheep arrives in the flock and attracts the attention of Shaun, who keeps the rest of the gang amused with his efforts to woo her(n)

programme.sub-title: Liza, Tyra and Hamza: Liza Makes Blueberry Pancakes
programme.desc: In this programme we visit three children who are all having a fascinating day out. In Russia, seven-year-old Liza picks blueberries with her grandma - and helps turn them into delicious pancakes. In Ghana, Tyra, who's five, goes to the market with her mum and learns how to do the shopping. And in Jordan, Hamza, who's eight, has a day out in town with his dad, and buys a lovely present for his baby sister, Mimi. We see that although children live in different countries, may look different and speak different languages, the joys and discoveries of childhood are universal.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Britain's betting industry is booming, with the amount people gamble having almost doubled in a decade and company profits soaring to £14.5 billion. But are the bookies doing enough to protect problem gamblers? Reporter Bronagh Munro investigates an industry where complaints are rising even more quickly than profits, meeting one person who gambled away more than £3 million, and families who have lost their children to addiction.(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Oscar-winning actress Kate Winslet follows a rumour of Scandinavian ancestry on her late mother Sally's side of the family and is delighted when it turns out to be true and that she has Swedish heritage. However, her joy turns to tears and anger when she uncovers the extreme hardships her ancestors endured, from famine to flogging and imprisonment. Switching to her father's side of the family, Kate finds a drummer boy in the Grenadier Guards and, this time, an unusually positive encounter with prison(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the hospital drama with the staff of Holby's accident and emergency department as they deal with more sick and injured patients. The walls start to close in on Connie when the hospital board notices the high rate of benzodiazepine prescriptions in the emergency department, and she is forced to choose between her addiction and her career. Meanwhile, Iain takes a stand as he defends the paramedics in the emergency department, and Duffy learns that she does not need to be a nurse to be able to help others(n)

programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the opening round of Premier League fixtures, including West Ham United v Manchester City and Tottenham Hotspur v Aston Villa. programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: After the disastrous Open Day at his house, Phil is nowhere to be found. As Edith and Sandra hurry to Phil's house, Edith receives a phone call from her 'difficult' sister Joan inviting herself to stay. Sandra pulls faces, but Edith feels obliged to agree. The duo enter Phil's house where they find a piano-playing Queenie, but no Phil. Roger visits the recovering Bob in hospital and tells him that he has thought of a new way to make money and he has let his mother's spare room, online, to two Americans. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese and Sue Johnston(n)

credits.actor: Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese, Sue Johnston

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and her team rescue and revamp a green enamel light shade, a 1970s sideboard and a 1930s Lloyd Loom chair in the hope of making a profit(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a retired couple with a budget of £450,000 search for a rural retreat in West Wales. Whilst in the area, Jules also visits Lammas, Britain's first eco-village(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern try to help the Miller family in North Lincolnshire, with parents Wes and Mel wanting to save up for their wedding after 10 years together. With no savings and big expectations, they need to make considerable changes if they want the wedding of their dreams - can Alex and Steph show these big spenders that it is possible to create memories without breaking the bank, and help them put money aside for their special day? The Great British public gets their say too, as they put four similar-looking biker jackets to the test(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate what could turn out to be one of the best bargains ever seen on the programme. Almost 30 years ago, Bob Kay bought a junk shop still life for £1. If it turns out to be by Giorgio de Chirico, it could be worth up to £50,000. Fiona and Philip visit Rome, where de Chirico lived for most of his life, and while they learn a great deal about the painter's status as a master of 20th-century art, they also discover he is widely faked. However, they do come across some interesting documents with which to begin their investigation(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a celebrity version of the general knowledge quiz, featuring four teams of stars from the 1970s. The contestants are David Jensen, Les McKeown, Diane Keen, Martin Jarvis, Robin Askwith, Liza Goddard, Isla St Clair and Patti Boulaye(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team is in Perthshire, where Matt Baker visits Cultybraggan, a former PoW camp that is now a thriving community of artisans, craftspeople and food producers. Charlotte Smith jumps in a kayak and takes to the water on a wildlife safari, while Steve Borwn meets women growing tea in the south Highlands and learns the art of making the perfect cuppa(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A return visit to the historic Abbey Pumping station in Leicester, now the city's Museum of Technology, which complete with four working beam engines is a fine example of Victorian engineering. Expert Ronnie Archer-Morgan swoons over some 1960s steel sculptures, while books specialist Rupert Powell meets playwright Joe Orton's sister who brings in the typewriter on which he wrote some of his most important scripts. Plus, Amin Jaffer discovers a valuable collection of exotic metal boxes - they look like snuff boxes but their actual purpose is much more intriguing. Presented by Fiona Bruce(n)

programme.title: Poldark
programme.desc: When the banks start issuing paper notes instead of gold, Demelza finds herself thrown into uncharted waters with her workers and the locals, a situation taken advantage of by Tess and Jacka. Morwenna tackles the grief of being parted from her son, while Geoffrey Charles makes plain his intent to marry Cecily, landing both of them in trouble. George conspires once more against Ned and the Poldarks as he and his allies enact a plan to destroy their opposition for good. Drama, starring Aidan Turner(n)

programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the hospital drama with the staff of Holby's accident and emergency department as they deal with more sick and injured patients. The walls start to close in on Connie when the hospital board notices the high rate of benzodiazepine prescriptions in the emergency department, and she is forced to choose between her addiction and her career. Meanwhile, Iain takes a stand as he defends the paramedics in the emergency department, and Duffy learns that she does not need to be a nurse to be able to help others(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: After the disastrous Open Day at his house, Phil is nowhere to be found. As Edith and Sandra hurry to Phil's house, Edith receives a phone call from her 'difficult' sister Joan inviting herself to stay. Sandra pulls faces, but Edith feels obliged to agree. The duo enter Phil's house where they find a piano-playing Queenie, but no Phil. Roger visits the recovering Bob in hospital and tells him that he has thought of a new way to make money and he has let his mother's spare room, online, to two Americans. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese and Sue Johnston(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern try to help the Miller family in North Lincolnshire, with parents Wes and Mel wanting to save up for their wedding after 10 years together. With no savings and big expectations, they need to make considerable changes if they want the wedding of their dreams - can Alex and Steph show these big spenders that it is possible to create memories without breaking the bank, and help them put money aside for their special day? The Great British public gets their say too, as they put four similar-looking biker jackets to the test(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate what could turn out to be one of the best bargains ever seen on the programme. Almost 30 years ago, Bob Kay bought a junk shop still life for £1. If it turns out to be by Giorgio de Chirico, it could be worth up to £50,000. Fiona and Philip visit Rome, where de Chirico lived for most of his life, and while they learn a great deal about the painter's status as a master of 20th-century art, they also discover he is widely faked. However, they do come across some interesting documents with which to begin their investigation(n)

programme.desc: The team is in Perthshire, where Matt Baker visits Cultybraggan, a former PoW camp that is now a thriving community of artisans, craftspeople and food producers. programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A return visit to the historic Abbey Pumping station in Leicester, now the city's Museum of Technology, which complete with four working beam engines is a fine example of Victorian engineering. Expert Ronnie Archer-Morgan swoons over some 1960s steel sculptures, while books specialist Rupert Powell meets playwright Joe Orton's sister who brings in the typewriter on which he wrote some of his most important scripts. Plus, Amin Jaffer discovers a valuable collection of exotic metal boxes - they look like snuff boxes but their actual purpose is much more intriguing. Presented by Fiona Bruce(n)

programme.title: Poldark
programme.desc: When the banks start issuing paper notes instead of gold, Demelza finds herself thrown into uncharted waters with her workers and the locals, a situation taken advantage of by Tess and Jacka. Morwenna tackles the grief of being parted from her son, while Geoffrey Charles makes plain his intent to marry Cecily, landing both of them in trouble. George conspires once more against Ned and the Poldarks as he and his allies enact a plan to destroy their opposition for good. Drama, starring Aidan Turner(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Britain's betting industry is booming, with the amount people gamble having almost doubled in a decade and company profits soaring to £14.5 billion. But are the bookies doing enough to protect problem gamblers? Reporter Bronagh Munro investigates an industry where complaints are rising even more quickly than profits, meeting one person who gambled away more than £3 million, and families who have lost their children to addiction.(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Oscar-winning actress Kate Winslet follows a rumour of Scandinavian ancestry on her late mother Sally's side of the family and is delighted when it turns out to be true and that she has Swedish heritage. However, her joy turns to tears and anger when she uncovers the extreme hardships her ancestors endured, from famine to flogging and imprisonment. Switching to her father's side of the family, Kate finds a drummer boy in the Grenadier Guards and, this time, an unusually positive encounter with prison(n)

programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption assesses the US and UK's constitutional models. He describes Britain's unwritten constitution as a political institution. programme.desc: Lake Titicaca, straddling the border between Peru and Bolivia in the Andes Mountains, is one of South America's largest lakes. Living here are the Uro - an ancient people, described at times as the only decedents of the Incas. They are the people of the floating islands, a very small community of about 2,000 with a community consisting of 50 swimming islands. Every day from the furthest corners, the youngest inhabitants paddle their way in their small boats to school(n)

programme.title: Cuimhneachan (Remembrance)
programme.desc: The Gaelic songs and poetry of the First World War and the stories behind them. Featuring specially recorded performances by Gaelic singers Art Cormack, Linda MacLeod, Murdo MacDonald and Robert Robertson, as well as readings from both songs and poems. The first episode looks at what bards such as Neil MacDonald from North Uist and Duncan Johnston from Islay had to say at the beginning of the war, and later on after direct experience of trench warfare on the battlefields of France(n)

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin enjoys one of Britain's National Trails as she walks part of the Thames Path through Oxfordshire - meeting some of the people and animals who call the banks of this impressive river home. Ben Fogle is in Whitby on the North Yorkshire coast to follow one of the nation's favourite dishes - fish and chips - from sea to plate. The programme also catches up with Dales shepherdess Amanda Owen as her family celebrates dad Clive's birthday(n)

programme.title: Liar Liar
programme.desc: A lawyer, whose underhand ploys to win cases leave him little time to see his son, discovers he is incapable of lying after the boy's birthday wish makes his dad tell only the truth. However, this causes a host of problems as the attorney tries to muddle his way through a high-profile divorce settlement. Comedy, with Jim Carrey, Maura Tierney, Justin Cooper, Jennifer Tilly and Cary Elwes(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.desc: In a continuation of the series that started in January, Julia Bradbury explores some of her favourite wonders along Britain's varied coastline, which stretches for more than 19,000 miles. She lands on the Isle of Barra's beach airport and helps count the puffins of the Farne Islands. She also watches dive-bombing sea birds, bargains for freshly caught fish in Cornwall and investigates Dover Castle's vital role in defending the gateway to Britain(n)

programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo reaches the end of his journey in Santa Maria Di Leuca. He enjoys a tour of Italy's most southerly church, and cooks a saffron and chicken risotto for the priest and some of the nuns who work there. Gino also visits Leuca's famous waterfall, which was built by Mussolini and is only switched on for special occasions, before heading out by boat to see the end of Italy from the water. For his final dish, Gino cooks locally caught swordfish with rosemary sauteed potatoes and tangy gremolata(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Tommy Hicks of the Midsomer Yeomanry finally has his name added to the Midsomer Parva war memorial, though he was shot for cowardice at the Somme in the First World War. It becomes apparent that time has not healed the rift between his descendants and the residents of Hammond Hall, whose patriarch Duggie Hammond pulled the trigger on his comrade all those years ago. A drink-fuelled mock trial of retired colonel Henry Hammond leads to a killing, and Tom Barnaby has to unravel the tangle of bitterness linking property developer Dave Hicks's brood with their neighbours. Drama, guest starring Donald Sinden and George Cole, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Apollo 13
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about the ill-fated 1970 Apollo 13 mission to the moon. Crew leader Jim Lovell is faced with a nightmare when the mission suffers an on-board explosion - leaving the team stranded in space with a dwindling oxygen supply while flight controllers and engineers in Houston try to find a way to bring the crew safely back to Earth. With Tom Hanks, Kevin Bacon, Bill Paxton, Ed Harris and Gary Sinise(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A mother of two is found murdered in her back garden. Vera and Aiden investigate the case, and delve into the disturbing secrets and ongoing conflicts that lurk beneath the surface of the victim's seemingly peaceful suburban community. The case takes a more complicated turn when the dead woman's estranged family make an unexpected reappearance, and while several of her neighbours may have had cause to carry out a crime of passion, her relatives may have had compelling motives of their own to kill. Guest starring Rita Tushingham(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Airport
programme.desc: The extended summer heatwave causes the tarmac of the runway to melt, and operations manager Jamie has to ensure the damage is repaired as quickly as possible. programme.title: Rescue: River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: Documentary focused on rescue efforts across the British Isles, featuring access to Mountain Rescue teams, Coast Guard and air ambulances. In the first edition, a rescue team in Kerry is called to Carrantuohil, the highest mountain in Ireland, to rescue an American couple who have got lost and are experiencing some of the worst weather Ireland can produce. On the Humber estuary, the Humber Inshore Lifeboat is called to a reported body on the banks of the river, but the job turns out to be a false alarm(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell follow three stories of siblings brought together after decades apart. programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo reaches the end of his journey in Santa Maria Di Leuca. He enjoys a tour of Italy's most southerly church, and cooks a saffron and chicken risotto for the priest and some of the nuns who work there. Gino also visits Leuca's famous waterfall, which was built by Mussolini and is only switched on for special occasions, before heading out by boat to see the end of Italy from the water. For his final dish, Gino cooks locally caught swordfish with rosemary sauteed potatoes and tangy gremolata(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Tommy Hicks of the Midsomer Yeomanry finally has his name added to the Midsomer Parva war memorial, though he was shot for cowardice at the Somme in the First World War. It becomes apparent that time has not healed the rift between his descendants and the residents of Hammond Hall, whose patriarch Duggie Hammond pulled the trigger on his comrade all those years ago. A drink-fuelled mock trial of retired colonel Henry Hammond leads to a killing, and Tom Barnaby has to unravel the tangle of bitterness linking property developer Dave Hicks's brood with their neighbours. Drama, guest starring Donald Sinden and George Cole, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Apollo 13
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about the ill-fated 1970 Apollo 13 mission to the moon. Crew leader Jim Lovell is faced with a nightmare when the mission suffers an on-board explosion - leaving the team stranded in space with a dwindling oxygen supply while flight controllers and engineers in Houston try to find a way to bring the crew safely back to Earth. programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An elderly man reports an unusual break-in, during which the uninvited visitor left a mysterious statue on the kitchen table. It soon emerges that it is just the first in a series of unexplained events, as a police phone box arrives and then disappears from his garden, cement is delivered to his house for no apparent reason and a fire is started in his garage. Noakes advises Steve to keep well away as it is not technically a police matter, but the officer decides to investigate further. Rural drama, starring Trevor Ray, Georgie Glen and James Carlton(n)

credits.actor: Trevor Ray, Georgie Glen, James Carlton

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Vernon buys a batch of topsoil for his new compost-producing business, little suspecting it is from a contaminated landfill site. The shady dealer who sold him the soil winds up dead - as do a number of local cows - and Vernon is soon fighting for his life. Elsewhere, seductive sergeant Nokes sets her sights on Steve Crane, and Bellamy wonders whether Gina will return to Aidensfield and marry him. Geoffrey Hughes, James Carlton and Mark Jordon star(n)

credits.actor: Geoffrey Hughes, James Carlton, Mark Jordon

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A philandering husband is found dying by the swimming pool after being shot during a house party at the home of Lady Lucy Angkatell. Legendary Belgian Sleuth Poirot, who is guest of honour at the gathering, appears to have an open-and-shut case on his hands - but the investigator finds himself up against one of his finest adversaries. Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet, Lysette Anthony and Edward Fox(n)

credits.actor: Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), David Suchet, Lysette Anthony, Edward Fox

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team are in Hemel Hempstead, Hertfordshire, where they meet deaf couple Asif Iqbal and wife Sabina. The green-fingered experts attempt to turn a boring lawn and crumbling patio into a Bollywood dream garden with an outdoor kitchen for Asif, Sabina and their family(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Nurse Maggie Simkiss cares for end of life patients and treats their emotional well-being as well as their direct medical needs. To say thank you for her hard work, Alan Titchmarsh and his team of green-fingered experts arrive at her Hull home and help transform the unkempt garden into a grown-up Mediterranean hideaway(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings(n)

credits.actor: David Suchet, Lesley Manville, Alex Jennings

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The mysterious death of a former police commissioner forces a reluctant Morse to team up with old professional rival Chief Inspector Dawson. As they and Sergeant Lewis begin to investigate, a link is uncovered to the unsolved case of a girl murdered 18 years previously. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Kenneth Colley and Christopher Eccleston(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: After reaching breaking point with being followed around by Buddy, Martin resolves to find a vet to have it put down, while Ruth finds out that Bert has been trying to make his own whisky. Clive is suspicious of Mrs Tishell and who she might be cooking meals for, Penhale makes a hash of asking Janice out, and Louisa and Martin's therapy homework results in a picnic on the beach, where they are interrupted by local vet Angela Sim - who appears to be hallucinating. Comedy drama, guest starring Caroline Quentin(n)

credits.actor: Caroline Quentin

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When a spate of petty thefts culminates in a series of murders at a student hostel, Miss Lemon persuades the Belgian sleuth to investigate. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Philip Jackson, Pauline Moran, Damian Lewis and Paris Jefferson(n)

credits.actor: David Suchet, Philip Jackson, Pauline Moran, Damian Lewis, Paris Jefferson

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian tells the story of Tulip, a 10-week-old puppy abandoned in a graveyard close to the Battersea Old Windsor site. With hopes high that Tulip can be rehomed, Paul is devastated to learn there might be something wrong with her front legs. Plus, there's the potential for lively Labrador Scooby to be a search-and-rescue dog, but only if he can pass his exams(n)

programme.title: Tonight at the Palladium
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the variety show, featuring the cast of Beautiful: The Carole King Musical, with a medley of songs from the show, as well as former Westlife singer Markus Feehily and one-time Strictly dancers Vincent Simone and Flavia Cacace. Hans Klok, who describes himself as the world's fastest illusionist, teaches Jimmy a few tricks, there's comedy from Rob Beckett, and further music from Rebecca Ferguson and 1980s chart stars Deacon Blue(n)

programme.title: The Theory of Everything
programme.desc: Biopic of Stephen Hawking, exploring the renowned astrophysicist's romance with future wife Jane during their time at university in the 1960s and his initial diagnosis with motor neurone disease, which doctors believed would lead to his death within two years. Undaunted by deteriorating health, he continued his groundbreaking research into the origins of the universe. Starring an Oscar-winning Eddie Redmayne, with Felicity Jones and David Thewlis(n)

credits.actor: an Oscar-winning Eddie Redmayne, Felicity Jones, David Thewlis

programme.title: Cilla
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith takes the lead role in Jeff Pope's three-part drama charting Cilla Black's rise to fame in the 1960s. As the series opens, Priscilla White is a Liverpool typist with dreams of escaping the office and becoming a singer. It looks like her chance has come when she meets songwriter Bobby Willis, who claims to be in the music industry - so she's less than impressed to later discover his flash car is hired and he actually works in a bakery. Despite this inauspicious start, Cilla agrees to let him manage her, but will their arrangement still stand after her friends, a local band by the name of the Beatles, help her land an audition with genuine impresario Brian Epstein? Aneurin Barnard, Ed Stoppard and John Henshaw also star(n)

credits.actor: Aneurin Barnard, Ed Stoppard, John Henshaw also

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Animal Orphans
programme.desc: The comedian begins another tour of Zambia, Namibia and South Africa helping to hand-rear orphaned animals and prepare them for release back into their natural habitats. His first stop is the Moholoholo Wildlife Rehabilitation Centre, where he meets an eight-month-old rhino which lost its mother when she was killed by poachers. Next, he teaches a young aardvark how to hunt for termites, before providing some much-needed company for a lion cub which was raised as a pet until she got too big to handle(n)

programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. programme.sub-title: The Mystery of the Blue Train

Passengers include oil magnate Rufus Van Aldin (Elliott Gould) and his daughter Ruth (Jaime Murray) - to whom he has given a magnificent ruby known as the Heart of Fire. With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. programme.desc: Using two mini-submarines, a group of historians, wreck detectives, scientists and D-Day veterans go on an expedition to search the seabed for items from the Normandy landings of June 1944. Among the objects the team finds are a collection of American Sherman tanks that were specially adapted to float, and the wreck of the SS Leopoldville, a troopship sunk by a U-boat in December 1944 with the loss of more than 800 lives. Richard Armitage narrates(n)

programme.desc: Documentary using recorded figures and statistics to outline the full extent of the conflict, explaining the horrors of war and how it ever came to take place. The programme begins by looking at the story of Franz Honiok, a 43-year-old farmer who is often considered the first victim of the Second World War, before going on to show that when Germany invaded Poland in August 1939, no-one was ready for war(n)

programme.title: Schindler's List

He decides to try to save Jews destined for the concentration camps by employing them in his factory - an act of mercy made possible by a deal struck with a sadistic German commandant. Steven Spielberg's Oscar-winning drama, starring Liam Neeson, Ben Kingsley and Ralph Fiennes(n)

programme.title: Bad Tenants, Rogue Landlords
programme.desc: The documentary about landlord and tenancy issues catches up on some of the stories featured so far. Emmanuel reveals what happened after he returned from a year working abroad to find himself £24,000 in mortgage arrears and unable to get into his own property. Andy made a mistake when he rented out his north London home to someone he knew, only for his so-called friend to sublet the property, pocket the profits and pay Andy nothing. What has happened since?(n)

programme.title: Bad Tenants, Rogue Landlords
programme.desc: Catching up with previous stories to find out what happened next. Pensioners John and Irene bought and rented out a house, only for their tenants to stop paying rent and move in their friends. The property ended up in a terrible state of repair - but worst of all, the landlords faced verbal threats and behaviour. Plus, the story of a tanning salon owner who offered an employee his home to rent, only for her to stop paying and turn it into a party house(n)

programme.title: Bad Tenants, Rogue Landlords
programme.desc: Updates on stories featured in previous episodes, including Kent couple Rachel and Chris, who were pushed to the limit when their relationship with their tenant broke down and she stopped paying rent, leaving them more than £5,000 out of pocket. Plus, a young mum locked out of her home by the young couple she allowed to stay there for free while the wife had a baby, and a landlord who reduced her tenants' rent when they got into financial difficulties - only for the payments to end altogether(n)

programme.title: Bad Tenants, Rogue Landlords
programme.desc: An edition catching up with previous cases. Owed thousands in rent, landlord Matthew decided to evict his tenant, and called on an eviction specialist for help. As the tenant lived with seven cats, Matthew was worried about the state of the property, but that turned out to be the least of his problems. Cameras catch up with Matthew to see if he turned his property around. Having found a buyer for his house, Kirk asked the sitting tenant to leave. However, on the day of the move, she refused, stopped paying rent and Kirk's sale fell through. Kirk had to evict his nightmare tenant before he lost the house. Was he able to sell his property at least, or was he tempted to become a landlord once again?(n)

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: The Colemans from Salisbury swap homes, budgets and lives with the Morgan family from Hampshire, who are intrigued to know what life is like for the top 10-per-cent earners in the country. The Colemans have a large four-bedroom house, enjoy watersports and music festivals and have a weekly budget of about £1,800. The Morgans, meanwhile, who live in a second-floor flat, get by on £158 a week(n)

programme.title: Rich Kids Go Skint
programme.desc: Observational series following rich kids who move in with families struggling to make ends meet(n)

programme.title: Summer Body: The Big Fat Lies About Diet & Exercise
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the potential pitfalls of trying to lead a more healthy lifestyle, from the dangers of crash dieting to the unexpected side effects of doing too much exercise. The programme features contributions from nutritionists, dieticians, GPs, cardiologists, fitness trainers and psychologists, who explain the health benefits of fat and carbohydrates, the addictive quality of sugar, the hazards of diet pills, and what can happen to someone who eats too many bananas(n)

programme.title: My Crazy Rich Asian Wedding

programme.title: Great Artists
programme.desc: Historian Tim Marlow explores the life and work of 17th-century visionary Dutch artist Rembrandt Harmenszoon van Rijn, who was famed for his skilful handling of light and shade in paintings portraying the human form. Born in Leiden in 1606, the eighth of nine children, his passion for art led him to an apprenticeship with a local painter, and from the time he was a 19-year-old he had a studio in Amsterdam, where he produced 75 self-portraits. Demand for his talents soon grew and success came when he was commissioned to paint The Anatomy Lesson of Dr Tulp(n)

programme.title: Traffic Cops

programme.title: The Secret World of Your Rubbish
programme.desc: Workers at Crapper and Sons salvage what they can from the hundreds of tons of plastic that is brought in every month while 10,000 tons of compost is sold to local farmers every year. S Norton is a multi-million pound complex of hydraulic and electrical systems, where hundreds of people shred 1.5 million tons of steel per year, which is then shipped off to Turkey for use in steelworks around the world. Today(n)

programme.title: Eaten By an Escalator: When Machines Bite Back
programme.desc: Documentary scrutinising people's darkest fears surrounding escalators by take a look at calamities involving the moving staircases and hearing from those who have been victims of accidents Plus, the programme debunks some myths and assesses the safety of these machines with industry experts in an attempt to allay widespread fears surrounding the dangers of escalators(n)

programme.title: Police Code Zero: Officer Under Attack
programme.desc: Programme exploring the rising number of assaults on police officers, revealing what they face in the line of duty, and the emotional toll these incidents take. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

Things get going in the afternoon during a beatboxing contest, although old tensions resurface at dinner and the hosts are left to calm things down. A massive spread is laid on at breakfast, but when one guest takes issue with the cooked items, Lesley and Chloe worry what impact it will have come payment day(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. David and Ewa Woodward want to challenge Martin's criticism over the way they run their business. Next, Donna Taylor wants to find out more about the issue that caused Martin and Sara to lose sleep. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

In the waiting room, a woman is comforted by her ex-boyfriend while she awaits treatment for a cut finger(n)

programme.title: It Was Alright In
programme.desc: Matt Lucas returns to narrate another light-hearted retrospective poking fun at Britain's viewing habits in the 1970s. This time, he focuses on the world of comedy, unfettered as it was to the conventions of political correctness. A selection of clips from hit TV shows illustrate how, if viewers were not straight, white, middle-class men, they were at risk of being offended, and highlights examples from On the Buses, The Two Ronnies, Dave Allen at Large and The Wheeltappers and Shunters Social Club. Includes commentary and contributions by Melvyn Hayes, Eric Chappell, Bill Oddie, Krishnan Guru-Murthy, Grace Dent, Nish Kumar, Ellie Taylor, Joel Dommett and Phil Wang(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

In the waiting room, a woman is comforted by her ex-boyfriend while she awaits treatment for a cut finger(n)

programme.desc: In 2010, Phil Spencer helped 18-year-old student Claire, who was looking for a wheelchair-accessible flat on London's South Bank with a view of the river. Claire had £1.7million to spend, gained from a personal injury settlement. In 2012, Kirstie Allsopp was searching south London for a flat for Jen. She had spent the previous year in Kabul, Afghanistan, and had high expectations of what she could get for her budget. Now Phil catches up with both to see how they are getting on(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two sets of first-time buyers who are struggling to find homes. In Southampton, Jenny and Tom are keen to move out of their cramped rental property and into a house of their own, but they are facing fierce competition and a fast market. Meanwhile, in the New Forest, Georgie and Will are desperate to live together after seven years of being a couple. However, with Will working in Belgium during the week, the pair are finding suitable properties are snapped up before they have even had the chance to view them(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.desc: Tutor Kathy Anderson hosts the third dinner party from Portsmouth and hopes to impress her guests with a Gallic-inspired menu. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At The Bluebell Woods in Northampton, David and Donna Bletsoe Brown try to bring a touch of class to their woodland B&B, but Fay Lahiani and Ray Cooper are on the warpath and are quick to discover a mucky mattress. Meanwhile, Marc Harris and Wayne Williams reveal their competitive streak during an afternoon of horse riding(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to The Lion Hotel in Pontypool for the third day of the contest, where manager Marc Harris and head chef Wayne Williams aim to deliver a high end service. Although some of the guests are impressed by the rooms, Ray and Fay find it too small for the price, and simmering tensions between some of the competitors leads to a clash over dinner. At breakfast, Wayne's food comes under fire, but it is David and Donna's feedback about finding a nail in their room that really rattles their hosts(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

Wayne and Marc are determined to be brutally honest, and Donna and David are forced to work closely with their rivals Fay and Ray during an afternoon team-building exercise. Over dinner, Damon admits that taking on the B&B, pub and restaurant all by himself may have not been the best decision - as proved when he has trouble running a solo breakfast service(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time, with Fay and Ray from Shiralee B&B wanting to find out why David and Donna were so critical of their cleanliness. The group members continue to air their grievances as they find out what they've been paid, before the winner is revealed(n)

programme.title: Savage Kingdom
programme.desc: Animals battle for domination of hunting grounds in a remote region of Africa, beginning with a leopard who faces new challenges when she has to protect her two cubs(n)

programme.title: Shocking Emergency Calls
programme.desc: A family trip to the Cincinnati Zoo turns into chaos and horror as a three-year-old falls into the enclosure of Harambe, the silverback gorilla(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?

Crewe officers Billy Elliot and Greg Greaves help a man who claims someone else has moved into his flat while he was on holiday, while in Warrington, PC Jon Tetlow investigates reports of a man frightening children. A background check reveals numerous reports of problems between the man and his neighbours, so police have to act quickly before locals take matters into their own hands(n)

programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to the coastal community of Long Beach, California, to save an Italian restaurant that was opened in 1958 and now under the misguided leadership of Nino, who blamed by brother Michael and sister Carina for the eatery's failings. programme.title: Food Unwrapped

programme.title: Jamie's Comfort Food Bites

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
