programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Four pairs of B&B owners from around Britain take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at the Grange in Oxfordshire, where Graham Neil and his wife Cherry offer a picture-framing course and a morning bagpipe wake-up call. The competing guests are from Ceredigion, Edinburgh and Abingdon-on-Thames(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition reaches its second day and the group visits the village of Llandysul, Ceredigion, to enjoy the hospitality of Katie Clamp and husband Andy. Edinburgh guest-house manager Tony Costa is not happy to find dog hairs on his bed, and Graham Neil is unimpressed after taking a dip in the hot tub. Things seem to get worse when efforts to prepare a hearty breakfast backfire, leading to chaos in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Tony and Dawn Ann Costa own the five-star Amar Agua in Edinburgh and pride themselves on their high standards. They decide to entertain their guests with a meal at a leading restaurant and a visit to a subterranean museum, but Katie Clamp keeps complaining and the hosts struggle to keep calm(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Terry and Peter Boswell welcome the group to their Abbey Guest House in Abingdon-on-Thames, Oxfordshire. They organise a day trip to the Thames, but Tony and Katie are still feuding, and the situation only gets worse at dinner as the evening ends in tears(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The four sets of owners discover how the other competitors rated their businesses by opening the cash-filled envelopes, containing what their rivals thought they should receive. They meet for one last time to learn who has won the best value B&B of the week(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp meets newlyweds Graeme and Anna, who have relocated from Oxfordshire to Yorkshire only to find their hopes of buying a big, spacious property in a rural location thwarted by a rising market. Phil Spencer helps local couple Sammi and Tom locate their perfect property on a budget of £180,000 in a specific three-mile radius north of Leeds(n)

programme.title: Love It or List It Australia
programme.desc: Andrew Winter and Neale Whitaker help Margie and Grayson, whose dated house in Jindalee, Queensland is in need of some serious modernisation(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Chester's Penguin Island is home to one of the most enduring love stories in the zoo. At 22, Rud is the oldest on the island, and has been with his partner, Spike, for over 15 years. However, keepers are concerned that Rud's arthritic hip could be affecting his quality of life. Meanwhile, the Sumatran tigers are upgrading to a luxury new enclosure on the other side of the zoo as part of Chester's huge expansion. But they've lived in their home for most of their lives so it's not going to be an easy move(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team return for an eleventh series. In the first episode, a tiny Jackahuahua puppy is in need of emergency spinal surgery after being attacked by a dog in the park, but it is not certain that he will be able to get back on his feet again after the operation. Elsewhere, a Bernese has a rare cyst in his elbow, requiring a challenging operation for removal, and an excitable Labrador requires elbow replacement surgery, which will result in a long and difficult road to recovery(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Series following a British family restoring a French chateau. As summer arrives, Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree are busier than ever with seven weddings and 14 food lovers' weekends to prepare for. And as if that wasn't enough to keep them busy, they must also sort out the garden, work on a stables conversion and embark on a surprising floating adventure(n)

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Prunella Scales and Timothy West embark on a final canal journey in the first of two programmes exploring Vietnam and Cambodia. The trip begins in the limestone islands of Halong Bay, before travelling along the Red River to Hanoi and the ancient citadel of Hoa Lu. They also attend the famous lantern festival in the trading port of Hoi An(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 77-year-old former motorbike racer arrives with a potentially deadly swelling of the main artery that could burst at any moment. His only option is major surgery, which could be dangerous at his age. A woman whose boyfriend was killed in a cycling accident a year previously is also injured while on her bike, and a medical technician and a mobile caterer talk about their roles in aiding people to recovery(n)

programme.title: Fatberg Autopsy: Secrets of the Sewers
programme.desc: Under the streets of Britain's cities an epidemic of fatbergs plagues the sewers. These monster blockages of congealed fat and human waste are a product of what people have flushed down our toilets and sinks. Presenter Rick Edwards joins a team of scientists and sewer workers to perform the first fatberg autopsy - taken from the biggest blockage ever found in the UK, at east London's Abbey Mills Pumping Station. Pathology technician Carla Valentine and experts analyse the contents to discover the chemistry, biology and toxicology of this beast from the sewer(n)

programme.title: Top Gear

The journey begins at Nardo, a high-speed testing ground so vast it can be seen from space, and includes a nerve-racking drive in Rome before reaching its conclusion with a timed challenge against the Stig at the Imola circuit. programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actress Sheridan Smith, who went from singing with her parents in the working men's clubs near their North Lincolnshire home to successful stage performances in Little Shop of Horrors and Legally Blonde - not to mention her countless TV appearances - embarks on a journey to find out where her family's musical talents come from. She is soon hot on the trail of her great-great-grandfather Benjamin Doubleday, a world-class banjo player and musical impresario - but she is surprised by his change in fortunes(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Food writer Mary Berry is one of Britain's best-loved TV cooks, finding fame thanks to her judging role on The Great British Bake Off. A self-confessed go-getter, she wants to trace her family tree to know where she gets her energy and drive from. The journey begins in Bath, where Mary grew up, and takes her to Norwich, where both her grandparents lived. Along the way she uncovers ancestors including a bankrupt book-binder, a self-motivated corset-maker and a third who worked in a much more familiar profession(n)

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: In the second programme, the historian explores how science and detection influenced the popular culture of murder during Victorian times. Writers including Charles Dickens and Wilkie Collins were fascinated by grisly crimes, and the literary genre that came out of it captured the imagination of readers. The presenter also reveals that when Jack the Ripper began his reign of terror in London at the same time Robert Louis Stevenson's Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde was published, the idea of the serial killer was introduced to the British public(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: The life of Mary Magdalene, a key figure in Christianity, having witnessed Jesus' death and resurrection(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates conflicting accounts of the killing of Italian dictator Benito Mussolini in 1945(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates historical evidence of the story of Jesus, examining his execution and his relationship with Mary Magdalene(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.sub-title: The Stasi: Behind the Berlin Wall
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston looks behind the hidden remnants of the Berlin Wall, a notorious symbol of division, to tell the story of the Stasi, East Germany's State Security Service(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: The nature series looks at animals that live on some of the world's highest mountains. Cameras show gelada baboons screaming to frighten off predators in Ethiopia, demoiselle cranes struggling to migrate over the Himalayas, snow leopards hunting on the peaks of Pakistan and a wild giant panda nursing a baby in a hillside cave. Presented by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough focuses on animals living in deserts. Cameras explore the dry Atacama of Chile, where guanacos survive by licking dew off cacti, and capture the rare sight of wild camels eating snow to stay hydrated on Mongolia's Gobi plains. In the rocky Namibian desert, elephants desperately search for food and lions wander the wilderness hunting oryx(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Camera crews follow the activities of wildlife in the Arctic and Antarctic, including an emperor penguin colony's nine-month migration across sea ice to its breeding ground. The team also takes aerial and underwater shots to capture humpback whales feeding and tracks a male polar bear as it swims 100km to find new ice from which to hunt seals(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: The nature series looks at animals that live on some of the world's highest mountains. Cameras show gelada baboons screaming to frighten off predators in Ethiopia, demoiselle cranes struggling to migrate over the Himalayas, snow leopards hunting on the peaks of Pakistan and a wild giant panda nursing a baby in a hillside cave. programme.title: Secrets of Britain
programme.desc: The series examining some of the UK's landmarks continues, with a look at the history of subterranean London, uncovering Roman remains, plague pits and secret bunkers under the capital's streets(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Britain
programme.sub-title: Secrets of Her Majesty's Secret Service
programme.desc: The documentary series exploring the less well known side of Britain continues, with this edition exploring MI6, asking whether the shadowy world of espionage and counter-intelligence operations bear any resemblance to the James Bond novels(n)

programme.title: Royals on the Frontline
programme.desc: The highs and lows of Prince Harry and Prince William's roles in the forces, featuring an interview with Harry in which he discusses a planned secret deployment in Afghanistan(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Castle
programme.desc: Documentary chronicling a year in the existence of 1,000-year-old Windsor Castle and its inhabitants, from the housekeeper and chefs to the Queen herself, who has allowed her public and private life to be documented more extensively than ever before. The state visit of former President of France Jacques Chirac sees the staff organise a banquet and a performance of a West End musical inside the building - but protesters outside threaten to mar proceedings(n)

programme.title: The True Story of the Mary Celeste
programme.desc: On December 4, 1872, the Mary Celeste was found adrift in the Atlantic with its cargo fully intact, yet with no one on board. Experts use CGI and fresh evidence in a bid to solve the mystery of the ship and learn how and why its crew disappeared(n)

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: How the Americans, once they were provoked into war, out-produced the rest of the world so quickly and by such a huge margin, including the likes of General Motors(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actress Sheridan Smith, who went from singing with her parents in the working men's clubs near their North Lincolnshire home to successful stage performances in Little Shop of Horrors and Legally Blonde - not to mention her countless TV appearances - embarks on a journey to find out where her family's musical talents come from. She is soon hot on the trail of her great-great-grandfather Benjamin Doubleday, a world-class banjo player and musical impresario - but she is surprised by his change in fortunes(n)

programme.title: Napoleon
programme.desc: Andrew Roberts' three-part profile of the French emperor concludes as the historian sheds light on Bonaparte's eventual downfall. He explores how a breach of trust by Tsar Alexander I led Napoleon to launch his ultimately disastrous 1812 invasion of Russia, and began a chain of events that would lead to the erosion of his support across the continent, culminating in his decisive defeat at the hands of the Duke of Wellington during the Battle of Waterloo. Andrew reveals how the monarchies of Europe banded together to bring down the French emperor, and then visits the remote Atlantic island of St Helena, where the British eventually imprisoned Napoleon following his defeat in the Netherlands(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Inspector George Gently and The Professionals star Martin Shaw is the latest celebrity to go in search of his roots, and he's particularly interested in finding out more about his paternal grandfather Edwin, who was rarely spoken about by his family. The actor has only one photo of his ancestor, which shows him aged roughly 17 and wearing a military uniform. By zooming in on the picture, Martin's brother was able to identify their grandfather's badge, which suggests he was in the Royal Marines. Now, by meeting with a military historian, Martin tries to fill in the rest of the story, and in the process learns more about Edwin's role in defending Birmingham against German air assaults during the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: DJ, actor and presenter Reggie Yates sets out to trace his ancestry. His mother and father were both born in Ghana, but he never knew his grandparents, and he is keen to know where his surname comes from, believing it might be Dutch. However, as he looks further back, his family tree springs some unexpected surprises(n)

programme.title: Catching History's Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: An insight into the development of forensic science as a way of catching murderers over the past 200 years, charting the scientific breakthroughs that have changed the course of justice. Surgeon and writer Gabriel Weston begins by looking at the difficulty of identifying the body in a murder case, from charred bones to bodies completely dissolved in acid. She focuses on the use of teeth and bite marks to identify a victim or killer, traces the uses of insects to pinpoint the time of death, and meets the geneticist who pioneered the technique of DNA profiling(n)

programme.sub-title: The House of the White Queen
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team head to Groby Old Hall in Leicestershire, formerly the home of Edward IV's wife Elizabeth Woodville, and discover a surprising array of archaeological remains beneath its gardens, including a medieval wall and possible evidence of a Norman castle. They are joined by author Philippa Gregory, who delves into the complex history of the Greys, the house's former owners and one of the most powerful dynasties in medieval Britain(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team travel to Jersey, where they search for the origins of Mont Orgueil Castle, an imposing fortress constructed during the reign of King John. Their excavation is complicated by a newer castle that was built on top of the old foundations in Tudor times - and the entire site is located on a rocky outcrop with sides so steep they can only be scaled by mountaineers(n)

programme.desc: As the summer nears its end, a Londoner's celebrity night at their bar in the south of France seems set be a wash-out when the heavens open. A couple from Kent face bad news about their application for a glamping site in Spain(n)

programme.title: Coast vs Country
programme.desc: Kirsty Duffy searches the Welsh countryside to find Debbie and Paul properties for their £350,000 budget, while Kerr Drummond scours the north Wales coast to find their dream home(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Pregnant rhino Asha goes into labour overnight, meaning there is a surprise waiting for the keepers when they arrive to work the next morning. Meanwhile, Malayan tapirs Margery and Betong say goodbye to their daughter, Solo, who is leaving to start a new life at a zoo in Germany, and female dead leaf mantis Minnie is looking for her first partner(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island. The no-nonsense chef struggles to persuade hot-headed owner Abby to make his suggested changes, and a confrontation results in him being thrown out of the building in the middle of a dinner service. The eatery's co-owner and financier begs Gordon to return, but his success hinges on his ability to win Abby over(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for the Grasshopper Also in Carlstadt, New Jersey, a family-owned Irish restaurant. There, he lends a helping hand to Mitch Sandler, who has been struggling to live up to the expectations of his father-in-law since he invested heavily in the business. The chef also tries to improve the presentation of dishes emerging from the kitchen(n)

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George Clarke helps the owners of an Edwardian family home with an unfortunate combination of wall-to-wall 1970s panelling, an indestructible cement staircase and a boxy bathroom shoved into a bay window. He also helps a couple update their Tudor cottage by knocking two rooms together to create an opulent master en suite(n)

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Helicopter pilot Paul, 59, is embarking on his first-ever date dressed in women's clothing and hopes that 51-year-old Sydney can see that underneath he is 'just a normal bloke', while make-up artist and mother-of-five Bianca, 31, sits down for a meal with white van man and fellow single parent Daniel(n)

programme.title: Sean Lock: Keep It Light
programme.desc: The 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown captain captured live on stage in a stand-up performance, talking about everything from the price of cinema food, accusations of him having a mid-life crisis, his behaviour on the internet and jewellery heists(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Joe Wilkinson take on Jon Richardson and Sara Pascoe in the words-and-numbers quiz, while Sam Simmons joins wordsmith Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters(n)

programme.title: The Lateish Show with Mo Gilligan
programme.desc: The RTS award-winning and Bafta-nominated comedian and actor chews the fat with big-name guests including actor Danny Dyer, broadcaster Maya Jama, comedian Joe Lycett and singer-songwriter Kelis. Plus, exciting games, hilarious sketches and superstar performances(n)

programme.title: Jade: The Reality Star Who Changed Britain
programme.desc: Ten years on from her death, this series explores the rollercoaster life of Jade Goody, one of the most loved - and hated - reality TV stars, to tell a bigger story of class, politics and cultural change in Britain, at a time when reality TV became the biggest and most polarising form of entertainment in the country. Featuring exclusive interviews and never-before-seen footage, the series explores how Jade's life story mirrors a period of astonishing social change in Britain(n)

programme.title: I Am
programme.desc: A single woman with a successful career (Humans' Gemma Chan) struggles with her mother's demands that she start a family before it is too late, and feels under further pressure as she watches her friends settle down and have children. Seeking a deeper meaning in her life, she goes on a series of disastrous dates that only leave her further mired in insecurity and anguish. She eventually meets James (Arinze Kene), whom she feels she can both love and trust - but remains uncertain whether they can have a future together(n)

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Stephen Mangan and Joan Bakewell host the painting challenge. Nine contestants paint celebrity sitters Jodie Comer, Daniel Lismore and Nick Moran, before the judges decide who deserves the last spot in the semi-final(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: A selection of previous investigations from the programme delving into the secrets behind mass-produced food. Jimmy Doherty heads to Kenya to find out what makes one tea more expensive than another, before meeting a taster for one of the UK's largest tea producers. Matt Tebbutt braves the icy waters of the North Sea to join divers harvesting scallops by hand from the sea bed, while Kate Quilton is in Israel to learn why some limes are much juicier than others(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Competitive golf-loving couple Lynn and Mike from Southampton have been holidaying in Spain for 40 years and even lived there previously. They love it so much they now wish to now buy a holiday home there, so Danni Menzies lines up five options for their £60,000 budget(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Tamsin is looking for her dream holiday home on the Greek island of Crete. She and her best friend Emma, who she has known since meeting at nursery school, have enjoyed many a holiday on the island, and now Tamsin is ready to have her own bolthole there. Laura Hamilton works within her budget of £100,000 to find a suitable property(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman is on hand to help couple Tracey and Dean search for a holiday home in the Mar Mentor in Spain with a budget of £70,000(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas helps house-hunters Taz and Tracy from London, who are looking for a property on Cyprus for £70,000 or less(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Posh Pawnbrokers
programme.desc: In Penzance, pawnbroking bosses Larry Kohen and Neil McLaughlin are doing deals on a pair of unusual hand-painted unicorn sculptures, a 200-million year old fossil and a rare gold disc by '70s pop star Olivia Newton-John. At London Road, head honcho Dan Hatfield handles a staggering amount of gold jewellery with a huge price tag, and a unique piano created for a concert by the award-winning folk rock band Mumford & Sons(n)

programme.title: Posh Pawnbrokers

programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Boston to help change the fortunes of a failing Italian restaurant run by two brothers. The eatery's decline began when one of the pair started embezzling money to fund his prescription drug addiction, and things only got worse when the he was sentenced to four years in prison. With the time now served, he has returned, but must first regain his brother's trust before he can save their business(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.desc: The chef travels to New Orleans to visit Zeke's, a much-loved restaurant that has seen standards slip since the original owner died. The new proprietors, husband and wife duo Darryl and Ellen Cortello, focused all the efforts on the financial side of things, so it is up to Gordon to show them that they first need to love food to make any profit(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

The guests she's out to impress are 46-year-old year farmer Turid, 30-year-old comedy promoter Naz, 28-year-old gas and oil purchasing specialist Sylvian and 21-year-old economics student David. But dessert turns out to be sticky in more ways than one; cue tears before bedtime from the host(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

Naz is, by his own admission, not the greatest cook and things don't go according to plan and much of his food is burned and difficult to chew. programme.desc: Nils and Martin save Akka from a great danger. Nils gets all the praise and Martin becomes jealous(n)

programme.title: Mud Men
programme.desc: Steve Brooker and Johnny Vaughan explore the site of the 1917 Silvertown munitions factory explosion, and join the crew of HMS St Albans to sample life in the Navy. They also travel back to the Second World War to experience the Blitz and find out about working on the front line of London's top-secret defences(n)

programme.title: Operation Amsterdam
programme.desc: A stiff-upper-lipped British Army officer engages in a spot of larceny to aid the war effort. Ordered to Amsterdam, he joins forces with two cunning Dutchmen and steals a secret cache of diamonds from a bank vault, thus preventing the gems falling into Nazi hands. Second World War adventure, starring Peter Finch, Tony Britton and Eva Bartok(n)

credits.actor: Peter Finch, Tony Britton, Eva Bartok

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Documentary investigating scientific breakthroughs in research on Alzheimer's disease, which are bringing hope to millions of sufferers across the world. New scanning and gene technology is allowing scientists to identify the disease at its earliest stages, often 15 years before symptoms appear and brain cells are destroyed, with drug trials in Colombia, USA and Europe showing startling success. The programme also reveals the changes in lifestyle that can prevent the development of the disease, as well as the effects of a UK-wide trial(n)

programme.title: The Purple Plain
programme.desc: A Canadian squadron leader's increasingly strange behaviour begins to worry the officers of an RAF station deep in the Burmese jungle. There is further concern when the unstable pilot crashes his plane miles from the base, forcing him to make an exhausting trek back to safety. Second World War drama, starring Gregory Peck, Bernard Lee, Brenda de Banzi and Maurice Denham(n)

credits.actor: Gregory Peck, Bernard Lee, Brenda de Banzi, Maurice Denham

programme.title: The Traitors
programme.desc: British and American secret agents join forces to unmask Communist spies plotting to steal top secret plans. Thriller, starring Patrick Allen and Jacqueline Ellis(n)

credits.actor: Patrick Allen, Jacqueline Ellis

programme.title: Hip Hop to Opera Holland Park
programme.desc: A documentary by Opera Holland Park which follows the journey of eight south London teenagers into the alien world of opera, bringing together varied characters and challenging perceptions(n)

programme.title: Heyday TV
programme.desc: A retrospective review of Bowie's classic album, featuring extensive interviews with Woody Woodmansey and the late Trevor Bolder(n)

programme.title: Spotlight London
programme.desc: Interviews about arts and events showcasing London's vibrant culture scene(n)

programme.title: Mud Men
programme.desc: Steve Brooker and Johnny Vaughan search the banks of Rotherhithe and explore the rich history of shipbuilding and trade on the Thames. They also reveal the origins of the London Underground and get to grips with a 19th-century steam ship(n)

programme.title: Dukes, Hustlers and the Real Casino Royale
programme.desc: On his death in 2000, John Aspinall was best known for his love of wildlife, but in the 1960s he ran world-famous casino the Clermont Club in London's Mayfair. There he created a gambling world of glamour and excitement, with aristocrats, film stars, MPs and millionaire businessmen arriving nightly. However, it has been claimed that Aspinall's involvement with gangland boss Billy Hill led to cheating at the card tables - although Aspinall's family and friends deny any criminal activity. This film mixes testimony of club members with dramatic reconstructions to tell the story of the alleged con(n)

programme.title: The Princess and the Gangster
programme.desc: Exploring the association between Princess Margaret and underworld figure John Bindon, who are portrayed in reconstructions by Nichola Lindsay and Norman Murray. A chance meeting between the pair in Mustique led to rumours of compromising photographs and secret meetings at Kensington Palace(n)

programme.title: Hummingbird
programme.desc: A haunted special forces soldier returns to London from Afghanistan and assumes another man's identity, hoping to make a fresh start. He is drawn into the city's criminal underworld, where his skills prove valuable to the highest bidder, and plots revenge against the gangster who killed his girlfriend. Crime thriller, with Jason Statham and Agata Buzek(n)

programme.title: 198 Grand
programme.desc: Three hapless criminals from South London get mixed up in an underworld double-cross, and find themselves in possession of a large sum of money that puts their lives in danger. Crime drama, starring Andy Frith and Dean Kilbey(n)

credits.actor: Andy Frith, Dean Kilbey

programme.title: Food Junkies
programme.desc: John Quilter selects his favourite moments from the series that cuts straight to the core of London's food scene(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A woman suffering from psoriasis and a hip condition plans to retire with her husband to the healthier climes of the Dominican Republic. Zilpah Hartley shows them around a villa with a pool, a beachside house and a tropical garden property(n)

programme.title: News Rewind
programme.desc: A digest of news and events from across London from the past few days(n)

programme.title: Talking Points
programme.desc: Interviews on news and events from across London(n)

programme.title: Britain's Lost Routes with Griff Rhys Jones
programme.desc: The actor and five companions follow the route thousands of medieval pilgrims took from Holywell, Flintshire, to St Davids in Pembrokeshire. They forage for food, face fast-flowing rivers and sometimes walk barefoot in sackcloth as they come to understand the lengths people in the Middle Ages went to for their religious beliefs. The travellers also encounter grand manor houses, ancient churches and relics, while Griff explores how the British holiday has its roots in the observance of holy days(n)

programme.title: The Bridges That Built London with Dan Cruickshank
programme.desc: The historian explores the city's Thames crossings, discovering there is much more to them than simply a means of getting from one place to another. Beginning in the marshes of the estuary, he moves upstream to Vauxhall, where archaeologists have recorded London's oldest bridge, and also learns about the legend of London Bridge, before reaching journey's end at the Millennium Bridge near St Paul's Cathedral(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi helps father and son Richard and Rich Gansbuehler from Northampton search for a holiday home in Tarragona, northern Spain(n)

programme.title: Roman Britain from the Air
programme.desc: The Romans were the first to invade and occupy Britain and their architecture and way of life have left a legacy that has lasted 1,600 years. Christine Bleakley takes to a helicopter to view remains that are best seen from the air, while historian Michael Scott is on the ground to explore further what each sky-high vantage point reveals. Their journey takes them from London, across to Wales and north to Hadrian's Wall, visiting fascinating sites that include the best-preserved Roman legionary barracks anywhere in Europe, the 6,000-seat amphitheatre at Caerleon and town walls that stretch for more than a mile(n)

programme.title: Mud Men
programme.desc: Steve Brooker and Johnny Vaughan search for hidden treasures along the banks of the River Thames, using metal detectors and shovels to find artefacts that offer a new perspective on London's history. They begin by the Tower of London, where they investigate the military origins of a mysterious gold trumpet and reunite a 250-year-old musket ball with the gun that fired it(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A Bournemouth estate agent searches for a dream holiday home with his grown-up daughter, taking his 40 years' experience in the UK market to La Palma in the Canaries. The island offers the pair a variety of properties to choose from, including sea-view bungalows, tailor-made condos and colonial houses(n)

programme.title: Heyday TV

programme.title: Hell is Sold Out
programme.desc: An author long assumed dead makes a surprise return, only to find books he did not write are being published under his name. He discovers that another writer, claiming to be his widow, is passing off her own creations as his previously unseen work to get them published. Drama, starring Mai Zetterling, Herbert Lom and Richard Attenborough(n)

credits.actor: Mai Zetterling, Herbert Lom, Richard Attenborough

programme.desc: A jeweler's assistant places a bet on the horses in a desperate attempt to raise enough ready cash to buy the girl of his dreams a suitably impressive present. programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: The team returns to investigate more health stories, with Chris van Tulleken examining the intriguing idea that the way we prepare food could be damaging our health. Okay, so we all know that frying isn't as good for us as steaming, but a radical new experiment suggests that the health-conscious among us could be cutting calories by making simple adjustments to the way we prepare starchy foods. in fact, certain methods could make them healthier than anyone previously imagined. Plus, surgeon Gabriel Weston witnesses a new procedure giving new hope to paralysed people in America, and journalist Michael Mosley looks at evidence that suggests we should all be spending a sensible amount of time in the sun(n)

programme.title: Prince Charles' Other Mistress
programme.desc: Documentary film examining the life of Australian heiress Dale 'Kanga' Tryon, who was Camilla Parker Bowles' rival in the 1970s for the affections of the Prince of Wales. Expelled from the royal circle for speaking to the Press about her relationship with the Windsors, she suffered a breakdown that saw her sectioned under the Mental Health Act, and died in 1997(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation.
